subject: Maths
read_aloud: Parkrun is 5 kilometres. Can you count to 5? One for each kilometre!
correct_response: Well done! 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 kilometres! That's the whole parkrun! Jesse and Reuben, you're both amazing runners!
incorrect_response: Let's count together! 1 kilometre, 2 kilometres, 3 kilometres, 4 kilometres, 5 kilometres! Parkrun is 5km!
hint: Start at 1 and count each kilometre. 1, 2, 3...

subject: Maths
read_aloud: If you ran 2km and then 3km, how far did you run altogether?
correct_response: Fantastic! 2 plus 3 is 5km! That's the whole parkrun distance! Jesse and Reuben, what a team!
incorrect_response: Let's add them up! 2km then 3 more: 3, 4, 5. You ran 5km altogether! That's a full parkrun!
hint: Start at 2 and count on 3 more. 2... then 3, 4, 5!

subject: Maths
read_aloud: If 10 people are running parkrun and 3 finish before you, how many are behind you?
correct_response: Brilliant thinking! You came 4th, so 6 people are behind you! 3 in front + you + 6 behind = 10!
incorrect_response: Let's work it out! 3 finished before you, then there's you in 4th place. 10 - 3 - 1 = 6 people behind you!
hint: 3 people are in front. You're one person. So 3 + 1 = 4 people accounted for. How many are left from 10?
metacognitive_prompt: Did you remember to count yourself as well as the people who finished?

subject: English
read_aloud: What rhymes with RUN? Is it FUN or CAR?
correct_response: Yes! RUN and FUN rhyme! And parkrun IS fun! Well done!
incorrect_response: Listen: R-UN, F-UN. They both end in UN! FUN rhymes with RUN! And parkrun is always fun!
hint: RUN ends in UN. Which word also ends in UN? Say them both!

subject: Maths
read_aloud: You see 3 dogs at parkrun. Then 1 more dog arrives. How many dogs are at parkrun now?
correct_response: That's right! 3 dogs plus 1 more is 4 dogs! Parkrun dogs are the best cheering squad!
incorrect_response: Let's count! There were 3 dogs: 1, 2, 3. Then 1 more arrived: 4! There are 4 dogs now!
hint: Start at 3 and count 1 more. What comes after 3?

subject: English
read_aloud: Can you spell RUN? It has 3 letters. R-U-N!
correct_response: Perfect spelling! R-U-N! You can spell as well as you can run!
incorrect_response: RUN has 3 letters: R-U-N. Say each sound: Rrr-Uuu-Nnn. R-U-N!
hint: Sound it out: Rrr is the first sound, Uuu is in the middle, and Nnn is at the end.

subject: Maths
read_aloud: Jesse and Reuben both ran parkrun. That's 5km each. How many kilometres did they run in total?
correct_response: Amazing! 5km plus 5km is 10km! Jesse and Reuben ran 10 kilometres between them!
incorrect_response: Jesse ran 5km and Reuben ran 5km. 5 + 5 = 10km altogether! Double parkrun!
hint: 5 plus 5. What do two fives make? Think of your number bonds to 10!

subject: English
read_aloud: What sound does FAST start with? Fff-ast! Like running fast at parkrun!
correct_response: That's right! Fff for FAST! You're fast at phonics AND fast at parkrun!
incorrect_response: Listen: Fff-ast. The first sound is Fff! Like in FUN and FINISH!
hint: Fff-ast. Feel the air come through your teeth: Ffff...

subject: Maths
read_aloud: If parkrun starts at 9 o'clock and you finish in 40 minutes, what time do you finish?
correct_response: Excellent! 9 o'clock plus 40 minutes is 9:40! Great time-telling and great parkrun time!
incorrect_response: Start at 9 o'clock and count forward 40 minutes. The hour stays at 9, and the minutes go to 40. So it's 9:40!
hint: The hour hand is still at 9. The minute hand moves 40 minutes forward from 12. Where does it point?
metacognitive_prompt: How did you work that out? Did you count the minutes or think about the clock?

subject: English
read_aloud: What word means the opposite of START? At parkrun they say 'Ready, set, go!' and at the end you...
correct_response: Yes! The opposite of START is FINISH (or STOP)! Jesse and Reuben always finish their parkrun!
incorrect_response: The opposite of START is FINISH! At parkrun you START running and then you FINISH at the end! Well done for always finishing!
hint: When you begin parkrun, you START. When you cross the line at the end, you...
